mentions.note: Premier household brands (Kleenex, Scott, Kotex, Huggies), 13x earnings, 5.25% yield. Merging with KenView (JJ consumer); Michael Chu synergies galore.
Excellent long-term company, 5%+ yield; suffered from Scott toilet paper fire; merger with Capital View is fantastic deal; stock not yet rewarded.
Consumer staples with 5.25% yield; recent distributor fire at largest facility creates temporary headwind but strong management (Mike Shoe) and dividend support case for averaging down.
Consumer staples with dividend and buyback; essential to own if diversifying away from speculative tech; solid earnings and valuation.
Reports Tuesday; expected better quarter than P&G but merger with Ken View could keep stock range-bound. Warming up to name.
Ultimate soft goods company; consumer staple with yield; defensive diversification.
CEO Michael Shu doing fantastic job despite Ken View acquisition issues; 5% yield at 13x earnings valuation intriguing; hold position despite weakness from acquisition concerns.
